purpose: Revise prompt for a failed impact_level selection.
system_addendum:
SECTION YOU ARE REVISING: impact_level

If value_in_enum failed: pick exactly Critical / High / Medium / Low.

If blast_radius_consistent failed: re-read problem.examples and pick the level that matches the worst-case consequence described. Regulator/safety/irreversible → Critical. Customer-visible regression / executive escalation → High. Team friction / longer MTTR → Medium. Local annoyance → Low.

If parallel_with_siblings failed: align with the sibling pattern unless there's a clear reason this principle prevents a worse / lesser failure than its siblings.
user_template:
Revise this impact_level.

<problem><description>{{ problem_description }}</description><examples>{{ problem_examples }}</examples></problem>
<siblings_impact_levels>{{ sibling_impact_levels }}</siblings_impact_levels>
<failing_draft><impact_level>{{ candidate_impact_level }}</impact_level></failing_draft>
<rubric_scores>
  <value_in_enum score="{{ score_value_in_enum }}">{{ justification_value_in_enum }}</value_in_enum>
  <blast_radius_consistent score="{{ score_blast_radius_consistent }}">{{ justification_blast_radius_consistent }}</blast_radius_consistent>
  <parallel_with_siblings score="{{ score_parallel_with_siblings }}">{{ justification_parallel_with_siblings }}</parallel_with_siblings>
</rubric_scores>

Return the JSON object specified in the output contract.

schema.impact_level: string — one of: Critical / High / Medium / Low
